_comment: Causal chain data for AI→ICESCR analysis. Derived from higher-order-effects.md and differential-diagnosis.md.

meta.title: AI Economic Impact: Causal Chain Analysis
meta.description: Seven hypotheses evaluated through four orders of knock-on effects, converging on the Four Scarcities model and the ICESCR Article 13 pivot.

nodes.label: AI capability
nodes.sublabel: Near-zero marginal cost for software labor

nodes.label: Composite A
nodes.sublabel: H2 + H3 + H4 + H7, modulated by H6
nodes.detail: No single hypothesis survives alone. The economy experiences constraint removal (H2) triggering demand explosion (H3), which hits new bottlenecks (H4), distributed unevenly (H7), with quality erosion as a countervailing drag (H6).

nodes.label: Constraint Removal (H2)
nodes.sublabel: Software labor cost approaches zero

nodes.label: Jevons Explosion (H3)
nodes.sublabel: Cheaper software creates more demand

nodes.label: Bottleneck Migration (H4)
nodes.sublabel: New constraints emerge as old ones dissolve

nodes.label: Quality Erosion (H6)
nodes.sublabel: Volume increase degrades average quality

nodes.label: Bifurcated Economy (H7)
nodes.sublabel: AI benefits concentrate among adopters

nodes.label: Commoditization (H2.2)
nodes.sublabel: Software becomes infrastructure, not competitive advantage
nodes.detail: When software labor cost approaches zero, software itself commoditizes. Value migrates to what software cannot provide: judgment, specification, curation.

nodes.label: Attention Crisis (H3.2)
nodes.sublabel: Software abundance overwhelms human evaluation capacity
nodes.detail: When software production costs collapse, the bottleneck shifts from production to discovery. Humans cannot evaluate a million AI-generated options.

nodes.label: Energy/Infrastructure Strain (H3.3)
nodes.sublabel: Physical compute limits become binding
nodes.detail: Demand explosion hits physical limits: power grid capacity, semiconductor supply, data center construction. Energy becomes the binding constraint on AI capability.

nodes.label: Judgment Premium (H4.1)
nodes.sublabel: Human evaluative capacity becomes scarce and valuable
nodes.detail: When software handles execution, the scarce resource becomes the ability to evaluate whether execution produced the right result. Judgment cannot be automated without the thing being judged.

nodes.label: Energy Constraint (H4.3)
nodes.sublabel: Compute demand strains physical infrastructure
nodes.detail: Identified independently via H3 (Jevons expansion) and H4 (bottleneck migration). Convergence from two separate chains validates the finding.

nodes.label: Specification Bottleneck (H4.4)
nodes.sublabel: Defining what to build becomes the binding constraint
nodes.detail: AI can execute against a specification with high fidelity. The bottleneck shifts to upstream: humans must specify what should be built before AI can build it.

nodes.label: Quality Stratification (H6.4)
nodes.sublabel: Market segments by quality tier; premium emerges
nodes.detail: Quality erosion triggers market correction: users pay premium for curated, verified, high-quality outputs. Creates two-tier software ecosystem.

nodes.label: Value Migration Triad
nodes.sublabel: H2.2 + H4.1 + H4.4: value moves to judgment × specification × curation
nodes.detail: When software commoditizes (H2.2) and execution is automated, value concentrates in the three things AI cannot currently do for itself: evaluating output quality (judgment), defining goals (specification), and selecting from abundance (curation). Article 13 — the right to education — governs all three: they develop through practice and experience.

nodes.label: Energy-Quality Feedback
nodes.sublabel: H3.3/H4.3 + H6.4: energy cost selects for efficiency
nodes.detail: Energy cost creates selection pressure: bloated commodity software becomes expensive to run; efficient premium software gains cost advantage. Partial self-correction of quality erosion.

nodes.label: Attention Platform Recurrence
nodes.sublabel: H3.2 + H2.2 + H6.4: gatekeeper dynamics return
nodes.detail: Software abundance + commodity pricing + quality variance = users cannot evaluate options. Curation platforms re-emerge as gatekeepers. The platforms that solve the attention allocation problem capture disproportionate value.

nodes.label: Judgment-Diffusion Paradox
nodes.sublabel: H4.1 + H7: technology diffuses; judgment doesn't scale
nodes.detail: Technology eventually reaches everyone (historical pattern). But judgment capability — the new scarce resource — requires practice, mentorship, feedback loops, and time. The bifurcation axis shifts from AI access to judgment capability.

nodes.label: Four Scarcities
nodes.sublabel: Judgment · Specification · Curation · Energy
nodes.detail: The post-constraint economy reorganizes around four scarce resources: Judgment (can this work? is this good?), Specification (what should we build?), Curation (which of a million options?), Energy (physical compute substrate). The first three are cognitive; the fourth is physical. Article 13 governs the development of the first three.

nodes.label: Values / Meaning
nodes.sublabel: Speculative frontier: if cognitive constraints dissolve
nodes.detail: If AI eventually assists with judgment, specification, and curation, the residual binding constraint becomes values (what matters?), purpose (why act?), and meaning (what makes action worthwhile?). These anchor in the ICESCR preamble: recognition of the inherent dignity of the human person.
